content: The effective reproduction number (Rt) is the average number of secondary infections produced by an infected individual at time t during an outbreak, accounting for immunity, interventions, and behavior changes. Unlike R0 (which assumes complete susceptibility), Rt reflects real-time transmission dynamics. Rt changes as susceptible population decreases through infection or vaccination, and as interventions modify contact rates or transmission probability. When Rt > 1, cases increase; when Rt < 1, cases decline. Rt estimation uses case counts, hospitalization data, or death counts with statistical models accounting for reporting delays.
source: WHO outbreak analytics, CDC epidemiology principles
